english: a musical instrument, esp. used by the Boṅpo (bon po), Glr., γśaṅ (gshang) ₒkról-ba ('khrol ba) to play on that instrument Mil.
rough, rugged places or tracts Cs:
1. = śód-pa (shod pa) to comb. — 2. = ₒčád-pa ('chad pa) II., to explain, to relate.

1. the lower part of a thing, γśám-du (gsham du) a. adv. down, b. postp. under, below, beneath; also adv. farther down, more towards the end, in the course of; examples v. sub leu (le'u); postp. deï γśám-du (de'i gsham du) under it. — 2. barren, B. and col., mo-γśám (mo gsham), rgod-γśám (rgod gsham), ba-γśám (ba gsham) a barren woman, mare, cow.
Sch.: a certain style of writing.

1. also γśìn-po (gshin po) good, fine, žiṅ śin-pa (zhing shin pa) B., C. a fertile field, sa-ṗyógs γśin-pa (sa phyogs gshin pa) a rich country Stg.; γśin-par rmó-ba (gshin par rmo ba) to plough well; γśín-sa (gshin sa) 1. fertile field or land. 2. v. γśìn-po (gshin po). — 2. = γśím-pa (gshim pa).

one deceased, a dead man, γśìn-mo (gshin mo) a dead woman, e.g. *šin-mó jor-zóm* (*shin mo jor zom*) the deceased, the late Jorzóm; it may have reference to the body, as well as to the soul, or to both together. — γśìn-rje (gshin rje) the god of the dead, of the lower regions, of hades, also regarded as the judge of the dead, Ssk. यम; γśìn-rjei yśed (gshin rje'i gshed) a. id., Yama the destroyer. b. the destroyer of Yama, Siwa; Ssk. यमान्तक cf. also Schl. 93. *čin-dúd* (*cin dud*) knot, opp. to *ṭol-dúd* (*tol dud*) a bow, a slip-knot W. — γśìn-ₒdré (gshin 'dre) the soul as a ghost or spectre Sch. — γśìn-ₒprás (gshin 'pras) the convulsive motions, the writhings of a dying creature. — γśín-zas (gshin zas) food presented to the Lamas when a person has died (Cs.: food prepared, or exposed for the dead?). śín-sa (shin sa) 1. burying ground, cemetery, 2. fertile field.

also γśíg-pa (gshig pa), 1. Sch.: to range, to compare; Ld. to be ranged, to draw up in files; *gral γśíg rgyáb-pa* (*gral gshig rgyab pa*) C. to induce assembled people by means of a stick to stand or sit closer; cf. žal-bdág (zhal bdag). — 2. ral-grí γśíb-pa (ral gri gshib pa) Wdn.?
Sch. to be irresolute, unsettled in opinion; to be distrustful, suspicious.

often also bśis (bshis), 1. nature, temper, natural disposition, γśis-k̇a (gshis kha) col. id., raṅ-γśís yin (rang gshis yin) it is their nature, their natural disposition Mil. — γśís-kyis (gshis kyis) by the very nature of the case, without secondary causes, naturally, quite of itself Mil.; γśís-ṅán (gshis ngan) Mil., Do. was also explained by rgyú-med-par (rgyu med par), prob. implying merely: not having been one's self the efficient cause. — 2. person, body, γśís ṭams-čád-du ₒbyúg-go (gshis thams cad du 'byug go) they anointed the whole body Do.
Cs. to rebuke, reproach; the Lexx. explain it by: śúb-bur smád-pa (shub bur smad pa) to blame in a whisper, i.e. behind a person's back.
pf. γśes (gshes), to abuse, revile, with la (la), Dzl. and elsewh.; γśe yaṅ slar mi γśè-ba (gshe yang slar mi gshe ba) even when reviled, (one should) not revile again (rule for monks) Cs.

(imp. γśegs (gshegs), Dulva in Feer Introd. etc. p. 68; but śog (shog) is prob. the original and older form), Bal. *šags-čas* (*shags cas*), resp. to go, to go away, opp. to ₒbyón-pa ('byon pa) to come Glr.; in other passages to come Dzl.; γśegs-grábs mdzad (gshegs grabs mdzad) he made preparations for setting out Mil.; nám-mka-la ₒpúr-žiṅ γśegs (nam mkha' la 'phur zhing gshegs) he ascended to heaven Tar.; slar γśégs-pa (slar gshegs pa) to return, to come back. γśégs-pa (gshegs pa) is col. seldom used, but often in books, and mostly of Buddha and great saints; bdé-bar γśégs-pa (bde bar gshegs pa) to die, of saints and kings; sku γśegs-pa (sku gshegs pa) Glr.; dgúṅ-du (dgung du) or dgúṅ-la γśégs-pa (dgung la gshegs pa) Mil, mḱar γśégs-pa (mkhar gshegs pa) Glr., id.; bdé(-bar)-γśegs(-pa) (bde (-bar) -gshegs (-pa)) as partic. = सुगत Buddha; de-bžín-γśegs-pa (de bzhin gshegs pa) तथागत acc. to the explanation now generally accepted: he that
